variants.original_description: Swap tokens on Uniswap V3. Supports all ERC20 tokens on Ethereum mainnet and L2s. Automatically finds the best route and handles slippage protection.
variants.variant_description: Swap tokens on Uniswap V3. Supports all ERC20 tokens on Ethereum mainnet and L2s. Automatically finds the best route and handles slippage protection. Example: swap(fromToken='USDC', toToken='ETH', amount=1000, slippageTolerance=0.5) swaps 1000 USDC to ETH.

variants.original_description: Swap tokens using 1inch aggregator for best rates across DEXs.
variants.variant_description: Swap tokens using 1inch aggregator for best rates across DEXs. Note: This tool does NOT support limit orders, recurring swaps, or cross-chain bridges - use dedicated tools for those.

variants.original_description: Supply assets to Aave lending pool to earn interest.
variants.variant_description: Supply assets to Aave lending pool to earn interest. Parameters: asset (address, e.g. '0xA0b8...'), amount (string in wei, e.g. '1000000000000000000' for 1 ETH), onBehalfOf (address, recipient of aTokens).

variants.original_description: Supply assets to Compound III to earn interest and use as collateral.
variants.variant_description: Supply assets to Compound III to earn interest and use as collateral. Example: supply_collateral(asset='0xC02...WETH', amount='5000000000000000000') supplies 5 WETH as collateral.

variants.change_description: Added rate limit and chain constraints
variants.original_description: Get complete DeFi portfolio breakdown across all protocols and chains.
variants.variant_description: Get complete DeFi portfolio breakdown across all protocols and chains. Supports Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base. Rate limit: 100 requests/min. Returns balances, positions, and USD values.

variants.original_description: Monitor DeFi position health and liquidation risk across protocols.
variants.variant_description: Monitor DeFi position health and liquidation risk across protocols. Supports Aave, Compound, MakerDAO. Does NOT track simple wallet balances or NFTs - use portfolio tools for those.

variants.original_description: Bridge tokens across chains using Stargate's unified liquidity pools.
variants.variant_description: Bridge tokens across chains using Stargate's unified liquidity pools. Example: bridge_tokens(srcChainId=1, dstChainId=42161, token='USDC', amount='1000000000') bridges 1000 USDC from Ethereum to Arbitrum.

variants.original_description: Aggregate bridge quotes and execute optimal cross-chain transfers.
variants.variant_description: Aggregate bridge quotes and execute optimal cross-chain transfers. Aggregates routes from Stargate, Hop, Across, Celer. Supported chains: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, Base, BSC, Avalanche.

variants.original_description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system. Only works within allowed directories.
variants.variant_description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system. Only works within allowed directories. Does NOT support: binary files, files larger than 10MB, or paths outside the sandbox. Use binary_read for non-text files.

variants.original_description: Perform file operations including read, write, copy, move, delete on local filesystem.
variants.variant_description: Perform file operations including read, write, copy, move, delete on local filesystem. Parameters: operation (enum: 'read'|'write'|'copy'|'move'|'delete'), path (absolute or relative path), content (required for write), destination (required for copy/move).

variants.original_description: Fetches a URL from the internet and extracts its contents as markdown.
variants.variant_description: Fetches a URL from the internet and extracts its contents as markdown. Does NOT execute JavaScript - for dynamic/SPA pages, use browser-based tools like Firecrawl or Playwright instead.

variants.original_description: Scrape a single URL with Firecrawl. Handles JavaScript rendering and returns clean markdown.
variants.variant_description: Scrape a single URL with Firecrawl. Handles JavaScript rendering and returns clean markdown. Example: scrape_url(url='https://example.com/dynamic-page', formats=['markdown']) returns rendered page content.

variants.original_description: Performs a web search using the Brave Search API, returning relevant results.
variants.variant_description: Search the web using Brave Search API to find relevant results. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets for up to 20 results per query.

variants.original_description: AI-optimized search engine that returns concise, relevant answers.
variants.variant_description: AI-optimized search engine that returns concise, relevant answers. Modes: 'basic' (fast, 5 results) or 'advanced' (thorough, 10+ results with summaries). Best for factual queries requiring accuracy.

variants.original_description: Execute a SELECT query on the SQLite database.
variants.variant_description: Execute a SELECT query on the SQLite database. Read-only: does NOT support INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DDL statements. For write operations, use write_query tool instead.

variants.original_description: Execute analytical queries on ClickHouse for fast OLAP workloads.
variants.variant_description: Execute analytical queries on ClickHouse for fast OLAP workloads. Example: query(query='SELECT date, sum(revenue) FROM sales GROUP BY date ORDER BY date', format='JSON') returns aggregated time series.

variants.original_description: Send a message to a Slack channel.
variants.variant_description: Send a message to a Slack channel. Parameters: channel (string, e.g. '#general' or 'C1234567890'), text (string, supports Slack markdown and emoji :emoji:). Optional: thread_ts for replies.

variants.original_description: Send push notifications via ntfy.sh to any device.
variants.variant_description: Send push notifications via ntfy.sh to any device. Example: publish(topic='my-alerts', message='Build completed!', title='CI/CD') sends instant push notification to subscribed devices.

variants.original_description: Create a new event in Google Calendar.
variants.variant_description: Create a new event in Google Calendar. Parameters: summary (string, event title), start/end (ISO8601 datetime, e.g. '2024-03-15T14:00:00-07:00'), description (optional, supports HTML).

variants.original_description: Create a new task in Todoist with optional due date and priority.
variants.variant_description: Create a new task in Todoist with optional due date and priority. Example: create_task(content='Review PR #123', due_string='tomorrow 5pm', priority=2) creates a medium-priority task due tomorrow.
